Q Text: Preparation Download the database genealogy2021-10-19.sql, which has the following relational schema:     Scientist(id, name, university, year)    Teaches(advisor, student) In this schema, primary keys are underlined. The attributes advisor and student are foreign keys both referencing id in Scientist. The data was downloaded from the Mathematics Genealogy Project (https://www.genealogy.math.ndsu.nodak.edu/). We will reason about the data as an academic "family" tree where a scientist is a parent of the scientists (the children) they advised and taught during their career. Similarly, we may talk about a scientist's ancestors and descendants. Question Write an SQL query that selects (id, name, university, year) of all scientists that defended their PhD thesis at the 'Radboud Universiteit Nijmegen'  (Please note that the database uses the local, Dutch, spelling).
Answer: SELECT Scientist.id, Scientist.name, Scientist.university, Scientist.year FROM Scientist WHERE Scientist.university = 'Radboud Universiteit Nijmegen';

Q Text: Write an SQL query that selects (id, name, university, year) of all scientists that graduated from any university in Nijmegen, e.g. select universities that contain the string 'Nijmegen'.
Answer: SELECT id, name, university, year FROM Scientist WHERE university LIKE '%Nijmegen%';

Q Text: Give the SQL query that selects for each scientist their name, and the number of students they supervised, ordered descending by that count.
Answer: SELECT s.name AS scientist_name, COUNT(t.student) AS num_students_supervised FROM Scientist s LEFT JOIN Teaches t ON s.id = t.advisor GROUP BY s.id, s.name ORDER BY num_students_supervised DESC;

Q Text: Give the query that selects (id, name, university and year) of the advisor(s) of 'Desiderius Erasmus'
Answer: SELECT s.id, s.name, s.university, s.year FROM Scientist s JOIN Teaches t ON s.id = t.advisor JOIN Scientist erasmus ON t.student = erasmus.id WHERE erasmus.name = 'Desiderius Erasmus';

Q Text: Give the query that selects (id, name, university and year) of the student(s) of 'Desiderius Erasmus'
Answer: SELECT s.id, s.name, s.university, s.year FROM Scientist s JOIN Teaches t ON s.id = t.student JOIN Scientist erasmus ON t.advisor = erasmus.id WHERE erasmus.name = 'Desiderius Erasmus';

Q Text: Give the SQL query that selects (advisor, student) identifiers for which the advisor is the 'grandparent' of the student (so the student is the grandchild of the advisor).
Answer: SELECT t1.advisor AS grandparent, t2.student AS grandchild FROM Teaches t1 JOIN Teaches t2 ON t1.student = t2.advisor;

Q Text: Give a recursive SQL query that selects pairs of  (advisor, student) identifiers where the advisor is an ancestor of the student (and the student a descendant of the advisor) NB Recursive queries are supported by PostgreSQL, 9.0 or higher, MySQL 8.0 or higher, and SQLite 3.8.3 or higher.
Answer: WITH RECURSIVE AncestorDescendant AS ( SELECT advisor, student FROM Teaches   UNION    SELECT t1.advisor, t2.student FROM Teaches t1 JOIN AncestorDescendant t2 ON t1.student = t2.advisor )   SELECT advisor, student FROM AncestorDescendant;

Q Text: Write a (recursive) SQL query that selects (id, name, university, year) of all scientists that are descendants of 'Desiderius Erasmus' (so, Erasmus is an ancestor of each scientist)
Answer: WITH RECURSIVE Descendants AS ( SELECT id, name, university, year FROM Scientist WHERE name = 'Desiderius Erasmus'   UNION    SELECT s.id, s.name, s.university, s.year FROM Scientist s JOIN Teaches t ON s.id = t.student JOIN Descendants d ON t.advisor = d.id )   SELECT id, name, university, year FROM Descendants;

Q Text: Preparation Download the database genealogy2021-10-19.sql, which has the following relational schema:     Scientist(id, name, university, year)    Teaches(advisor, student) In this schema, primary keys are underlined. The attributes advisor and student are foreign keys both referencing id in Scientist. The data was downloaded from the Mathematics Genealogy Project (https://www.genealogy.math.ndsu.nodak.edu/). We will reason about the data as an academic "family" tree where a scientist is a parent of the scientists (the children) they advised and taught during their career. Similarly, we may talk about a scientist's ancestors and descendants. Question Write an SQL query that selects (id, name, university, year) of all scientists that defended their PhD thesis at the 'Radboud Universiteit Nijmegen'  (Please note that the database uses the local, Dutch, spelling).
Answer: SELECT id, name, university, year FROM Scientist WHERE university = 'Radboud Universiteit Nijmegen';

Q Text: Write an SQL query that selects (id, name, university, year) of all scientists that graduated from any university in Nijmegen, e.g. select universities that contain the string 'Nijmegen'.
Answer: SELECT id, name, university, year FROM Scientist WHERE university LIKE '%Nijmegen%';

Q Text: Give the SQL query that selects for each scientist their name, and the number of students they supervised, ordered descending by that count.
Answer: D SELECT S.name AS scientist_name, COUNT(T.student) AS nr_students_supervised FROM Scientist S LEFT JOIN Teaches T ON S.id = T.advisor GROUP BY S.id, S.name ORDER BY nr_students_supervised DESC;

Q Text: Give the query that selects (id, name, university and year) of the advisor(s) of 'Desiderius Erasmus'
Answer: SELECT S.id, S.name, S.university, S.year FROM Scientist S JOIN Teaches T ON S.id = T.advisor JOIN Scientist A ON A.id = T.student WHERE A.name = 'Desiderius Erasmus';

Q Text: Give the query that selects (id, name, university and year) of the student(s) of 'Desiderius Erasmus'
Answer: SELECT S.id, S.name, S.university, S.year FROM Scientist S JOIN Teaches T ON S.id = T.student JOIN Scientist A ON A.id = T.advisor WHERE A.name = 'Desiderius Erasmus';

Q Text: Give the SQL query that selects (advisor, student) identifiers for which the advisor is the 'grandparent' of the student (so the student is the grandchild of the advisor).
Answer: SELECT DISTINCT T1.advisor AS grandparent, T2.student AS grandchild FROM Teaches T1 JOIN Teaches T2 ON T1.advisor = T2.advisor WHERE T1.student != T2.student;

Q Text: Give a recursive SQL query that selects pairs of  (advisor, student) identifiers where the advisor is an ancestor of the student (and the student a descendant of the advisor) NB Recursive queries are supported by PostgreSQL, 9.0 or higher, MySQL 8.0 or higher, and SQLite 3.8.3 or higher.
Answer: WITH RECURSIVE Ancestors AS (   SELECT advisor, student   FROM Teaches     UNION     SELECT T.advisor, T.student   FROM Teaches T   JOIN Ancestors A ON T.advisor = A.student )   SELECT DISTINCT * FROM Ancestors;

Q Text: Write a (recursive) SQL query that selects (id, name, university, year) of all scientists that are descendants of 'Desiderius Erasmus' (so, Erasmus is an ancestor of each scientist)
Answer: WITH RECURSIVE Descendants AS (   SELECT S.id, S.name, S.university, S.year   FROM Scientist S   WHERE S.name = 'Desiderius Erasmus'     UNION     SELECT S2.id, S2.name, S2.university, S2.year   FROM Scientist S2   JOIN Teaches T ON S2.id = T.student   JOIN Descendants D ON T.advisor = D.id )   SELECT * FROM Descendants;

Q Text: Consider the following 2 transactions T1 and T2, which have read and write statements in a given execution order:     T1 : read1(x);  read1(y);  write1(x);  write1(y).    T2 : read2(x);  write2(x). Each read and write statement corresponds with an SQL SELECT or UPDATE statement, where x and y correspond to data items (in this case books with a certain ISBN number, i.e., the following queries on the table Book.     read1(x)  = SELECT title FROM Book WHERE isbn = '0136067018';    read1(y)  = SELECT title FROM Book WHERE isbn = '0321228383';    write1(x) = UPDATE Book SET title = title || ' part 1' WHERE isbn = '0136067018';    write1(y) = UPDATE Book SET title = title || ' part 1' WHERE isbn = '0321228383';     read2(x)  = SELECT title FROM Book WHERE isbn = ' 0136067018';    write2(x) = UPDATE Book SET title = title || ' part 2' WHERE isbn = ' 0136067018'; Question: Give an execution order of the read and write operations that is not serializable.
Answer Match: read2(x);  read1(x);  read1(y);  write1(x);  write1(y);  write2(x).

Q Text: Given that same transactions T1 and T2 as above, but now we explicitly add a commit statement for each transaction.     T1 : read1(x);  read1(y);  write1(x);  write1(y);  commit1(x,y)    T2 : read2(x);  write2(x);  commit2(x) Question: Give an execution order such that T2 does a dirty read.
Answer Match: read1(x);  read1(y);  write1(x);  write1(y);  read2(x);  write2(x);  commit2(x);  commit1(x,y).

Q Text: Give the query that selects (id, name, university and year) of the advisor(s) of 'Desiderius Erasmus'
Answer: SELECT Advisor.id, Advisor.name, Advisor.university, Advisor.year FROM Scientist, Scientist AS Advisor, Teaches WHERE Scientist.name = 'Desiderius Erasmus' AND Scientist.id = student AND Advisor.id = advisor;

Q Text: Give the SQL query that selects (advisor, student) identifiers for which the advisor is the 'grandparent' of the student (so the student is the grandchild of the advisor).

Q Text: Give a recursive SQL query that selects pairs of  (advisor, student) identifiers where the advisor is an ancestor of the student (and the student a descendant of the advisor) NB Recursive queries are supported by PostgreSQL, 9.0 or higher, MySQL 8.0 or higher, and SQLite 3.8.3 or higher.
Answer: WITH RECURSIVE Ancestor (parent,child) AS (    SELECT Ancestor.id AS parent, Descendant.id AS child    FROM Scientist AS Ancestor, Teaches, Scientist AS Descendant    WHERE NOT EXISTS (SELECT * FROM Teaches WHERE Descendant.id = advisor)    AND Ancestor.id = advisor    AND Descendant.id = student  UNION     SELECT Parent.id AS parent, Ancestor.parent AS child    FROM Scientist AS Parent, Teaches, Ancestor    WHERE Parent.id = advisor AND Ancestor.parent = student ) SELECT * FROM Ancestor ORDER BY Ancestor.child DESC;

Q Text: Write a (recursive) SQL query that selects (id, name, university, year) of all scientists that are descendants of 'Desiderius Erasmus' (so, Erasmus is an ancestor of each scientist)
Answer: WITH RECURSIVE Descendant(id) AS ( SELECT student AS id FROM Scientist, Teaches WHERE name = 'Desiderius Erasmus' AND id = advisor UNION SELECT student FROM Descendant, Teaches WHERE id = advisor ) SELECT Scientist.id, name, university, year FROM Scientist, Descendant WHERE Scientist.id = Descendant.id;

Q Text: Preparation Download the database genealogy2021-10-19.sql, which has the following relational schema:     Scientist(id, name, university, year)    Teaches(advisor, student) In this schema, primary keys are underlined. Question Write an SQL query that selects (id, name, university, year) of all scientists that defended their PhD thesis at the 'Radboud Universiteit Nijmegen'  (Please note that the database uses the local, Dutch, spelling).
Answer: SELECT id, name, university, year FROM Scientist WHERE university = 'Radboud Universiteit Nijmegen';

Q Text: Write an SQL query that selects (id, name, university, year) of all scientists that graduated from any university in Nijmegen, e.g. select universities that contain the string 'Nijmegen'.
Answer: SELECT id, name, university, year FROM Scientist WHERE university LIKE '%Nijmegen%';

Q Text: Give the SQL query that selects for each scientist their name, and the number of students they supervised, ordered descending by that count.
Answer: SELECT name, COUNT(student) FROM Teaches, Scientist WHERE Teaches.advisor = Scientist.id GROUP  BY name  ORDER BY COUNT(student)  DESC;

Q Text: Give the query that selects (id, name, university and year) of the advisor(s) of 'Desiderius Erasmus'
Answer: SELECT S1.id, S1.name, S1.university, S1.year FROM Teaches, Scientist AS S1, Scientist AS S2 WHERE S2.name = 'Desiderius Erasmus' AND S2.id=Teaches.student AND S1.id=Teaches.advisor;

Q Text: Give the query that selects (id, name, university and year) of the student(s) of 'Desiderius Erasmus'
Answer: SELECT S1.id, S1.name, S1.university, S1.year FROM Teaches, Scientist AS S1, Scientist AS S2 WHERE S2.name = 'Desiderius Erasmus' AND S2.id=Teaches.advisor AND S1.id=Teaches.student;

Q Text: Give the SQL query that selects (advisor, student) identifiers for which the advisor is the 'grandparent' of the student (so the student is the grandchild of the advisor).
Answer: SELECT DISTINCT ST.id, ST.name, ST.university, ST.year, S2.id, S2.name, S2.University, S2.year  From Teaches, Scientist AS ST, Teaches AS T1, Scientist AS S1, Scientist AS S2, Teaches AS T2 WHERE S1.id=T2.advisor AND T2.advisor = T1.student AND  S2.id= T2.student AND ST.id= T1.advisor;

Q Text: Give a recursive SQL query that selects pairs of  (advisor, student) identifiers where the advisor is an ancestor of the student (and the student a descendant of the advisor) NB Recursive queries are supported by PostgreSQL, 9.0 or higher, MySQL 8.0 or higher, and SQLite 3.8.3 or higher.
Answer: WITH RECURSIVE Ancestors AS ( SELECT Teach.advisor AS ancestor, Teach.student AS descendant, 1 AS depth FROM Teaches AS Teach UNION SELECT Old.ancestor, Teach.student, Old.depth + 1 FROM Ancestors AS Old JOIN Teaches AS Teach ON Old.descendant = Teach.advisor )  SELECT DISTINCT ancestor, descendant FROM Ancestors;

Q Text: Write a (recursive) SQL query that selects (id, name, university, year) of all scientists that are descendants of 'Desiderius Erasmus' (so, Erasmus is an ancestor of each scientist)
Answer: WITH RECURSIVE ErasmusAnchestor AS( SELECT advisor, student FROM Teaches WHERE advisor IN (SELECT id FROM Scientist WHERE name = 'Desiderius Erasmus') UNION SELECT teach.advisor, teach.student FROM Teaches AS teach JOIN ErasmusAnchestor  AS EA ON teach.advisor = EA.student ) SELECT DISTINCT stud.id, stud.name, stud.university, stud.year FROM ErasmusAnchestor AS EA JOIN Scientist AS stud ON EA.student = stud.id;

Answer: SELECT Scientist.id, Scientist.name, Scientist.university, Scientist.year FROM Scientist JOIN Teaches ON Teaches.advisor=Scientist.id JOIN Scientist AS Child ON Child.id=Teaches.student WHERE Child.name='Desiderius Erasmus';

Q Text: Give the query that selects (id, name, university and year) of the student(s) of 'Desiderius Erasmus'
Answer: SELECT Scientist.id, Scientist.name, Scientist.university, Scientist.year FROM Scientist JOIN Teaches ON Teaches.student=Scientist.id JOIN Scientist AS Advisor ON Advisor.id=Teaches.advisor WHERE Advisor.name='Desiderius Erasmus';

Q Text: Give the SQL query that selects (advisor, student) identifiers for which the advisor is the 'grandparent' of the student (so the student is the grandchild of the advisor).

Q Text: Give a recursive SQL query that selects pairs of  (advisor, student) identifiers where the advisor is an ancestor of the student (and the student a descendant of the advisor) NB Recursive queries are supported by PostgreSQL, 9.0 or higher, MySQL 8.0 or higher, and SQLite 3.8.3 or higher.
Answer: WITH RECURSIVE Ancestors(id) AS (     SELECT Ancestors.advisor, Teaches.student AS id     FROM Teaches     UNION     SELECT Teaches.advisor AS id     FROM Ancestors, Teaches     WHERE Ancestors.id=Teaches.advisor     AND NOT Teaches.advisor IS NULL ) SELECT * FROM Ancestors;

Answer Match: 1. T1 reads x (read1(x)). 2. T2 reads x (read2(x)). 3. T1 writes x (write1(x)). 4. T1 reads y (read1(y)). 5. T2 writes x (write2(x)). 6. T1 writes y (write1(y)).

Q Text: Preparation Download the database genealogy2021-10-19.sql, which has the following relational schema:     Scientist(id, name, university, year)    Teaches(advisor, student) In this schema, primary keys are underlined. The attributes advisor and student are foreign keys both referencing id in Scientist. The data was downloaded from the Mathematics Genealogy Project (https://www.genealogy.math.ndsu.nodak.edu/). We will reason about the data as an academic "family" tree where a scientist is a parent of the scientists (the children) they advised and taught during their career. Similarly, we may talk about a scientist's ancestors and descendants. Question Write an SQL query that selects (id, name, university, year) of all scientists that defended their PhD thesis at the 'Radboud Universiteit Nijmegen'  (Please note that the database uses the local, Dutch, spelling).
Answer: SELECT * FROM Scientist WHERE university='Radboud Universiteit Nijmegen';

Q Text: Write an SQL query that selects (id, name, university, year) of all scientists that graduated from any university in Nijmegen, e.g. select universities that contain the string 'Nijmegen'.
Answer: SELECT * FROM Scientist WHERE university LIKE '%Nijmegen%';

Q Text: Give the SQL query that selects for each scientist their name, and the number of students they supervised, ordered descending by that count.
Answer: SELECT s.name, count(*) AS count FROM Scientist s, Teaches t WHERE s.id=t.advisor GROUP BY s.name ORDER BY count DESC;

Q Text: Give the query that selects (id, name, university and year) of the advisor(s) of 'Desiderius Erasmus'
Answer: SELECT s2.* FROM Scientist s1, Scientist s2, Teaches t WHERE s1.name='Desiderius Erasmus' AND s1.id=t.student AND s2.id=t.advisor;

Q Text: Give the query that selects (id, name, university and year) of the student(s) of 'Desiderius Erasmus'
Answer: SELECT s2.* FROM Scientist s1, Scientist s2, Teaches t WHERE s1.name='Desiderius Erasmus' AND s1.id=t.advisor AND s2.id=t.student;

Q Text: Give the SQL query that selects (advisor, student) identifiers for which the advisor is the 'grandparent' of the student (so the student is the grandchild of the advisor).
Answer: SELECT DISTINCT t1.advisor, t2.student FROM Teaches t1, Teaches t2 WHERE t1.student=t2.advisor;

Each read and write statement corresponds with an SQL SELECT or UPDATE statement, where x and y correspond to data items (in this case books with a certain ISBN number, i.e., the following queries on the table Book.     read1(x)  = SELECT title FROM Book WHERE isbn = '0136067018';    read1(y)  = SELECT title FROM Book WHERE isbn = '0321228383';    write1(x) = UPDATE Book SET title = title || ' part 1' WHERE isbn = '0136067018';    write1(y) = UPDATE Book SET title = title || ' part 1' WHERE isbn = '0321228383';     read2(x)  = SELECT title FROM Book WHERE isbn = ' 0136067018';    write2(x) = UPDATE Book SET title = title || ' part 2' WHERE isbn = ' 0136067018'; Question: Give an execution order of the read and write operations that is not serializable.
Answer Match: read1(x); read1(y); write1(x); read2(x); write1(y); write2(x)

Q Text: Give the SQL query that selects for each scientist their name, and the number of students they supervised, ordered descending by that count.
Answer: SELECT name, COUNT (student) FROM Scientist S, Teaches T WHERE T.advisor = S.id GROUP BY S.name ORDER BY COUNT (student) DESC;

Q Text: Give the query that selects (id, name, university and year) of the advisor(s) of 'Desiderius Erasmus'
Answer: SELECT * FROM Scientist S, Scientist S2, Teaches T WHERE S2.name = 'Desiderius Erasmus' AND T.student = S2.id AND T.advisor = S.id;

Q Text: Give the query that selects (id, name, university and year) of the student(s) of 'Desiderius Erasmus'
Answer: SELECT * FROM Scientist S1, Teaches T WHERE T.student = S1.id AND T.advisor IN  (SELECT advisor  FROM Scientist S2  WHERE S2.name = 'Desiderius Erasmus' AND T.advisor = S2.id);

Q Text: Give the SQL query that selects (advisor, student) identifiers for which the advisor is the 'grandparent' of the student (so the student is the grandchild of the advisor).
Answer: SELECT DISTINCT T.advisor, T2.student FROM Teaches T, Teaches T2 WHERE T.student = T2.advisor;

Q Text: Give a recursive SQL query that selects pairs of  (advisor, student) identifiers where the advisor is an ancestor of the student (and the student a descendant of the advisor) NB Recursive queries are supported by PostgreSQL, 9.0 or higher, MySQL 8.0 or higher, and SQLite 3.8.3 or higher.
Answer: WITH RECURSIVE Ancestors(advisor, student) AS ( SELECT advisor, student  FROM Teaches UNION  SELECT T.advisor, A.student FROM Teaches T, Ancestors A WHERE T.student = A.advisor )  SELECT * FROM Ancestors;

Q Text: Write a (recursive) SQL query that selects (id, name, university, year) of all scientists that are descendants of 'Desiderius Erasmus' (so, Erasmus is an ancestor of each scientist)
Answer: WITH RECURSIVE Descendants(id, name, university, year) AS ( SELECT id, name, university, year FROM Scientist WHERE Scientist.name = 'Desiderius Erasmus' UNION SELECT S.id, S.name, S.university, S.year FROM Scientist S, Teaches T, Descendants D WHERE D.id = T.advisor AND S.id = T.student ) SELECT * FROM Descendants WHERE name != 'Desiderius Erasmus'

Answer: SELECT   S.name AS scientist_name,   COUNT(T.student) AS num_students_supervised FROM   Scientist S LEFT JOIN   Teaches T ON S.id = T.advisor GROUP BY   S.id, S.name ORDER BY   num_students_supervised DESC;

Q Text: Give the query that selects (id, name, university and year) of the advisor(s) of 'Desiderius Erasmus'
Answer: SELECT   S.id,   S.name,   S.university,   S.year FROM   Scientist S JOIN   Teaches T ON S.id = T.advisor JOIN   Scientist SE ON T.student = SE.id WHERE   SE.name = 'Desiderius Erasmus';

Answer: SELECT   S.id,   S.name,   S.university,   S.year FROM   Scientist S JOIN   Teaches T ON S.id = T.student JOIN   Scientist SE ON T.advisor = SE.id WHERE   SE.name = 'Desiderius Erasmus';

Q Text: Give a recursive SQL query that selects pairs of  (advisor, student) identifiers where the advisor is an ancestor of the student (and the student a descendant of the advisor) NB Recursive queries are supported by PostgreSQL, 9.0 or higher, MySQL 8.0 or higher, and SQLite 3.8.3 or higher.
Answer: WITH RECURSIVE AdvisorDescendants AS (   SELECT     T.advisor AS ancestor,     T.student AS descendant   FROM     Teaches T   WHERE     T.advisor IS NOT NULL     UNION ALL     SELECT     T1.advisor,     T2.student   FROM     AdvisorDescendants T1   JOIN     Teaches T2 ON T1.descendant = T2.advisor ) SELECT * FROM AdvisorDescendants;

Q Text: Write a (recursive) SQL query that selects (id, name, university, year) of all scientists that are descendants of 'Desiderius Erasmus' (so, Erasmus is an ancestor of each scientist)
Answer: WITH RECURSIVE Descendants AS (   SELECT     S.id,     S.name,     S.university,     S.year   FROM     Scientist S   WHERE     S.name = 'Desiderius Erasmus'     UNION ALL     SELECT     S.id,     S.name,     S.university,     S.year   FROM     Scientist S   JOIN     Teaches T ON S.id = T.student   JOIN     Descendants D ON T.advisor = D.id ) SELECT * FROM Descendants;

Q Text: Preparation Download the database genealogy2021-10-19.sql, which has the following relational schema:     Scientist(id, name, university, year)    Teaches(advisor, student) In this schema, primary keys are underlined. The attributes advisor and student are foreign keys both referencing id in Scientist. The data was downloaded from the Mathematics Genealogy Project (https://www.genealogy.math.ndsu.nodak.edu/). We will reason about the data as an academic "family" tree where a scientist is a parent of the scientists (the children) they advised and taught during their career. Similarly, we may talk about a scientist's ancestors and descendants. Question Write an SQL query that selects (id, name, university, year) of all scientists that defended their PhD thesis at the 'Radboud Universiteit Nijmegen'  (Please note that the database uses the local, Dutch, spelling).
Answer: SELECT id, name, university, year FROM Scientist WHERE university = 'Radboud Universiteit Nijmegen';

Q Text: Write an SQL query that selects (id, name, university, year) of all scientists that graduated from any university in Nijmegen, e.g. select universities that contain the string 'Nijmegen'.
Answer: SELECT id, name, university, year  FROM Scientist  WHERE university LIKE '%Nijmegen%';

Q Text: Give the SQL query that selects for each scientist their name, and the number of students they supervised, ordered descending by that count.
Answer: SELECT Scientist.name, COUNT(Teaches.student) AS n_students  FROM Scientist JOIN Teaches ON Scientist.id = Teaches.advisor GROUP BY Scientist.id, Scientist.name ORDER BY n_students DESC;

Q Text: Give the query that selects (id, name, university and year) of the advisor(s) of 'Desiderius Erasmus'
Answer: SELECT Scientist.id, Scientist.name, Scientist.university, Scientist.year  FROM Scientist  JOIN Teaches ON Scientist.id = Teaches.advisor  WHERE Scientist.name = 'Desiderius Erasmus';

Q Text: Give the query that selects (id, name, university and year) of the student(s) of 'Desiderius Erasmus'
Answer: SELECT Scientist.id, Scientist.name, Scientist.university, Scientist.year  FROM Scientist  JOIN Teaches ON Scientist.id = Teaches.student  WHERE Teaches.advisor = (    SELECT Scientist.id     FROM Scientist     WHERE Scientist.name = 'Desiderius Erasmus');

Q Text: Give the SQL query that selects (advisor, student) identifiers for which the advisor is the 'grandparent' of the student (so the student is the grandchild of the advisor).
Answer: SELECT Teaches1.advisor AS 'grandparent' AND Teaches2.student AS 'grandkid'  FROM Teaches Teaches1  JOIN Teaches Teaches2 ON Teaches2.student = Teaches1.advisor;

Q Text: Give a recursive SQL query that selects pairs of  (advisor, student) identifiers where the advisor is an ancestor of the student (and the student a descendant of the advisor) NB Recursive queries are supported by PostgreSQL, 9.0 or higher, MySQL 8.0 or higher, and SQLite 3.8.3 or higher.
Answer: WITH RECURSIVE Ancestor(advisor, student) AS (    SELECT advisor, student     FROM Teaches     UNION ALL  )  SELECT * FROM Ancestor;

Q Text: Write a (recursive) SQL query that selects (id, name, university, year) of all scientists that are descendants of 'Desiderius Erasmus' (so, Erasmus is an ancestor of each scientist)
Answer: WITH RECURSIVE Descendants(id, name, university, year) AS (    SELECT id, name, university, year     FROM Scientist     WHERE name = 'Desiderius Erasmus'     UNION ALL     SELECT S.id, S.name, S.university, S.year     FROM Scientist S     JOIN Teaches T ON S.id = T.student     JOIN Descendants D ON T.advisor = D.id  ) SELECT * FROM Descendants;

Q Text: Consider the following 2 transactions T1 and T2, which have read and write statements in a given execution order:     T1 : read1(x);  read1(y);  write1(x);  write1(y).    T2 : read2(x);  write2(x). Each read and write statement corresponds with an SQL SELECT or UPDATE statement, where x and y correspond to data items (in this case books with a certain ISBN number, i.e., the following queries on the table Book.     read1(x)  = SELECT title FROM Book WHERE isbn = '0136067018';    read1(y)  = SELECT title FROM Book WHERE isbn = '0321228383';    write1(x) = UPDATE Book SET title = title || ' part 1' WHERE isbn = '0136067018';    write1(y) = UPDATE Book SET title = title || ' part 1' WHERE isbn = '0321228383';     read2(x)  = SELECT title FROM Book WHERE isbn = ' 0136067018';    write2(x) = UPDATE Book SET title = title || ' part 2' WHERE isbn = ' 0136067018'; Question: Give an execution order of the read and write operations that is not serializable.
Answer Match: read1(x), read1(y), read2(x), write2(x), write1(x), write1(y)

Answer Match: read1(x), read1(y), write1(x), write1(y), read2(x), write2(x) commit1(x,y), commit2(x)

Answer: WITH RECURSIVE Ancestor(parent, child) AS (   SELECT advisor, student FROM Teaches   UNION   SELECT Ancestor.parent, Teaches.student   FROM Ancestor, Teaches   WHERE Ancestor.child == Teaches.advisor ) SELECT parent AS advisor, child AS student FROM Ancestor;

Q Text: Write a (recursive) SQL query that selects (id, name, university, year) of all scientists that are descendants of 'Desiderius Erasmus' (so, Erasmus is an ancestor of each scientist)
Answer: WITH RECURSIVE Ancestor(parent, child) AS (   SELECT advisor, student FROM Teaches   UNION   SELECT Ancestor.parent, Teaches.student   FROM Ancestor, Teaches   WHERE Ancestor.child == Teaches.advisor ) SELECT A.id, A.name, A.university, A.year FROM Scientist A, Scientist B, Ancestor WHERE Ancestor.child == A.id AND Ancestor.parent == B.id AND B.name == 'Desiderius Erasmus';

Q Text: Consider the following 2 transactions T1 and T2, which have read and write statements in a given execution order:     T1 : read1(x);  read1(y);  write1(x);  write1(y).    T2 : read2(x);  write2(x). Each read and write statement corresponds with an SQL SELECT or UPDATE statement, where x and y correspond to data items (in this case books with a certain ISBN number, i.e., the following queries on the table Book.     read1(x)  = SELECT title FROM Book WHERE isbn = '0136067018';    read1(y)  = SELECT title FROM Book WHERE isbn = '0321228383';    write1(x) = UPDATE Book SET title = title || ' part 1' WHERE isbn = '0136067018';    write1(y) = UPDATE Book SET title = title || ' part 1' WHERE isbn = '0321228383';     read2(x)  = SELECT title FROM Book WHERE isbn = ' 0136067018';    write2(x) = UPDATE Book SET title = title || ' part 2' WHERE isbn = ' 0136067018'; Question: Give an execution order of the read and write operations that is not serializable.
Answer Match: read1(x); read1(y); read2(x); write1(x); write2(x);  write1(y).

Q Text: Given that same transactions T1 and T2 as above, but now we explicitly add a commit statement for each transaction.     T1 : read1(x);  read1(y);  write1(x);  write1(y);  commit1(x,y)    T2 : read2(x);  write2(x);  commit2(x) Question: Give an execution order such that T2 does a dirty read.
Answer Match: read1(x); read1(y); write1(x); write1(y); commit1(x,y); read2(x); write2(x); commit2(x)

Answer: T1: Serializable and T2 Read Uncommitted

Answer: T1: Read Committed and T2 Read Committed

Q Text: Preparation Download the database genealogy2021-10-19.sql, which has the following relational schema:     Scientist(id, name, university, year)    Teaches(advisor, student) In this schema, primary keys are underlined. The attributes advisor and student are foreign keys both referencing id in Scientist. The data was downloaded from the Mathematics Genealogy Project (https://www.genealogy.math.ndsu.nodak.edu/). We will reason about the data as an academic "family" tree where a scientist is a parent of the scientists (the children) they advised and taught during their career. Similarly, we may talk about a scientist's ancestors and descendants. Question Write an SQL query that selects (id, name, university, year) of all scientists that defended their PhD thesis at the 'Radboud Universiteit Nijmegen'  (Please note that the database uses the local, Dutch, spelling).
Answer: SELECT * FROM Scientist  WHERE university='Radboud Universiteit Nijmegen';

Q Text: Write an SQL query that selects (id, name, university, year) of all scientists that graduated from any university in Nijmegen, e.g. select universities that contain the string 'Nijmegen'.
Answer: SELECT id, name, university, year FROM Scientist WHERE university LIKE '%Nijmegen%';

Q Text: Give the SQL query that selects for each scientist their name, and the number of students they supervised, ordered descending by that count.
Answer: SELECT name, count(*) as c FROM Scientist as s, Teaches as t Where t.advisor=s.id GROUP BY id, name ORDER BY c DESC;

Q Text: Give the query that selects (id, name, university and year) of the advisor(s) of 'Desiderius Erasmus'
Answer: SELECT id, name, university, year FROM Scientist WHERE id IN (SELECT advisor FROM Scientist as s, Teaches as t WHERE s.id=t.student AND name='Desiderius Erasmus');

Q Text: Give the query that selects (id, name, university and year) of the student(s) of 'Desiderius Erasmus'
Answer: SELECT id, name, university, year FROM Scientist WHERE id IN  (SELECT student FROM Scientist AS s, Teaches AS t WHERE s.id=t.advisor AND name='Desiderius Erasmus');

Q Text: Give the SQL query that selects (advisor, student) identifiers for which the advisor is the 'grandparent' of the student (so the student is the grandchild of the advisor).
Answer: SELECT DISTINCT t1.advisor, t2.student FROM teaches t1,teaches t2 WHERE t1.student=t2.advisor;

Q Text: Give a recursive SQL query that selects pairs of  (advisor, student) identifiers where the advisor is an ancestor of the student (and the student a descendant of the advisor) NB Recursive queries are supported by PostgreSQL, 9.0 or higher, MySQL 8.0 or higher, and SQLite 3.8.3 or higher.
Answer: WITH RECURSIVE Ance AS( SELECT DISTINCT t1.advisor, t1.student FROM teaches t1 UNION SELECT DISTINCT a.advisor,t2.student FROM Ance a, teaches t2 WHERE a.student=t2.advisor ) SELECT * FROM Ance;

Q Text: Write a (recursive) SQL query that selects (id, name, university, year) of all scientists that are descendants of 'Desiderius Erasmus' (so, Erasmus is an ancestor of each scientist)
Answer: WITH RECURSIVE Ance(id, name, university, year) AS ( SELECT s1.id, s1.name, s1.university, s1.year  FROM Scientist s1 WHERE id in  (SELECT student FROM Teaches t, Scientist s WHERE t.advisor=s.id AND s.name='Desiderius Erasmus') UNION SELECT s2.id, s2.name, s2.university, s2.year FROM Scientist s2 WHERE id IN (SELECT student FROM teaches t, Ance a WHERE t.advisor=a.id) ) SELECT * FROM Ance;

Question Write an SQL query that selects (id, name, university, year) of all scientists that defended their PhD thesis at the 'Radboud Universiteit Nijmegen'  (Please note that the database uses the local, Dutch, spelling).
Answer: SELECT * FROM Scientist WHERE university='Radboud Universiteit Nijmegen';

Q Text: Write an SQL query that selects (id, name, university, year) of all scientists that graduated from any university in Nijmegen, e.g. select universities that contain the string 'Nijmegen'.
Answer: SELECT * FROM Scientist WHERE university LIKE '%Nijmegen%';

Q Text: Give the SQL query that selects for each scientist their name, and the number of students they supervised, ordered descending by that count.
Answer: SELECT DISTINCT S.name, COUNT(*) AS Students FROM Scientist S, Teaches T WHERE S.id=T.advisor GROUP BY S.name ORDER BY Students DESC;

Q Text: Give the query that selects (id, name, university and year) of the advisor(s) of 'Desiderius Erasmus'
Answer: SELECT DISTINCT S.id, S.name, S.university, S.year FROM Scientist S, Teaches T WHERE S.id=T.advisor AND T.student=(SELECT id FROM Scientist WHERE name='Desiderius Erasmus');

Q Text: Give the query that selects (id, name, university and year) of the student(s) of 'Desiderius Erasmus'
Answer: SELECT DISTINCT S.id, S.name, S.university, S.year FROM Scientist S, Teaches T WHERE T.student=(SELECT student FROM Teaches WHERE advisor=(SELECT id FROM Scientist WHERE name='Desiderius Erasmus')) AND T.student=S.id;

Q Text: Give the SQL query that selects (advisor, student) identifiers for which the advisor is the 'grandparent' of the student (so the student is the grandchild of the advisor).
Answer: SELECT T.advisor, P.student FROM Teaches T, Teaches P WHERE T.student=P.advisor;

Answer: WITH RECURSIVE Ancestors(ancestor, student) AS (SELECT student, advisor AS ancestor FROM Teaches UNION  SELECT T.advisor AS ancestor FROM Teaches T, Ancestors A WHERE A.ancestor=T.student AND NOT T.advisor IS NULL) SELECT * FROM Ancestors;

Q Text: Write a (recursive) SQL query that selects (id, name, university, year) of all scientists that are descendants of 'Desiderius Erasmus' (so, Erasmus is an ancestor of each scientist)
Answer: WITH RECURSIVE Descendants(id) AS (SELECT student AS id FROM Teaches WHERE advisor=(SELECT id FROM Scientist WHERE name='Desiderius Erasmus') UNION  SELECT T.student AS id FROM Teaches T, Descendants D WHERE D.id=T.advisor AND NOT T.student IS NULL) SELECT DISTINCT S.id, S.name, S.university, S.year FROM Scientist S, Descendants D WHERE S.id=D.id;
